Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 49→ * 50→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 51→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 52→ * 53→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 54→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 55→ * 56→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 57→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 58→ * the filter is skipped. 59→ */ 60→class TemplateManager { 61→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 62→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 63→ companion object { 64→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 65→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 66→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 67→ 68→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 69→ } 70→ 71→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 72→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 73→ for (node in nodes) { 74→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 75→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 76→ } else { 77→ results.append( 78→ Pair( 79→ null, 80→ TemplateReplacement( 81→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 82→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 83→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 84→ ) 85→ ) 86→ ) 87→ } 88→ } 89→ 90→ return results 91→ } 92→ 93→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 94→ val value = tag.valueCase 95→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 96→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 97→ } else { 98→ TTSTag( 99→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 100→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 101→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 102→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 103→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 104→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 105→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 106→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 107→ ) 108→ } 109→ } 110→ 111→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 112→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 113→ } 114→ } 115→ } 116→ 117→ /** 118→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 119→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 120→ * using the _private fields directly. 121→ */ 122→ class TemplateRenderContext( 123→ card: Card, 124→ note: Note, 125→ browser: Boolean = false, 126→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 127→ template: JSONObject? = null, 128→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 129→ ) { 130→ private var _card: Card = card 131→ private var _note: Note = note 132→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 133→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 134→ 135→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 136→ 137→ companion object { 138→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 139→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 140→ } 141→ 142→ fun fromCardLayout( 143→ note: Note, 144→ card: Card, 145→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 146→ template: JSONObject, 147→ fillEmpty: Boolean 148→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 149→ return TemplateRenderContext( 150→ card, 151→ note, 152→ notetype = notetype, 153→ template = template, 154→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 155→ ) 156→ } 157→ } 158→ 159→ /** 160→ * Returns the card being rendered. 161→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 162→ * infinite loop. 163→ */ 164→ fun card() = _card 165→ 166→ fun note() = _note 167→ fun noteType() = noteType 168→ 169→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 170→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 171→ try { 172→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 173→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 174→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 175→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 176→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 177→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 178→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 179→ ) 180→ } 181→ 182→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 183→ val qtext = parseVideos( 184→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 185→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 186→ mediaDir 187→ ), 188→ mediaDir 189→ ) 190→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 191→ var qoutText = qout.text 192→ 193→ val atext = parseVideos( 194→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 195→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 196→ mediaDir 197→ ), 198→ mediaDir 199→ ) 200→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 201→ var aoutText = aout.text 202→ 203→ if (!_browser) { 204→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 205→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 206→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 207→ } 208→ 209→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 210→ questionText = qoutText, 211→ answerText = aoutText, 212→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 213→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 214→ css = noteType().getString("css") 215→ ) 216→ } 217→ 218→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 219→ val proto = col.run { 220→ if (_template != null) { 221→ // card layout screen 222→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 223→ _note.toBackendNote(), 224→ _card.ord, 225→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 226→ fillEmpty, 227→ true 228→ ) 229→ } else { 230→ // existing card (eg study mode) 231→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 232→ } 233→ } 234→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 235→ } 236→ 237→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 238→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 239→ var questionText: String, 240→ var answerText: String, 241→ val questionAvTags: List, 242→ val answerAvTags: List, 243→ val css: String = "" 244→ ) { 245→ 246→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 247→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 248→ } 249→ 250→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 251→ fun applyCustomFilters( 252→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 253→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 254→ frontSide: String? 255→ ): String { 256→ // template already fully rendered? 257→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 258→ return rendered[0].first!! 259→ } 260→ 261→ var res = "" 262→ for (union in rendered) { 263→ if (union.first != null) { 264→ res += union.first!! 265→ } else { 266→ val node = union.second!! 267→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 268→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 269→ node.currentText = frontSide 270→ } 271→ 272→ var fieldText = node.currentText 273→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 274→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 275→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 276→ } 277→ } 278→ 279→ res += fieldText 280→ } 281→ } 282→ return res 283→ } 284→ } 285→ 286→ /** 287→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 288→ * 289→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 290→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 291→ */ 292→ abstract class FieldFilter { 293→ abstract fun apply( 294→ fieldText: String, 295→ fieldName: String, 296→ filterName: String, 297→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 298→ ): String 299→ } 300→ companion object { 301→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 302→ } 303→} 304→ 305→/** 306→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 307→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 308→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 309→ * we use the webview player, like AnkiMobile does 310→ * 311→ * `file:///` is used to enable seeking the video 312→ */ 313→@NotInLibAnki 314→@VisibleForTesting 315→fun parseVideos(text: String, mediaDir: String): String { 316→ return SOUND_RE.replace(text) { match -> 317→ val fileName = match.groupValues[1] 318→ val extension = fileName.substringAfterLast(".", "") 319→ if (extension in VIDEO_EXTENSIONS) { 320→ val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, fileName).toString() 321→ val uri = getFileUri(path) 322→ 323→ @Language("HTML") 324→ val result = 325→ """""" 326→ result 327→ } else { 328→ match.value 329→ } 330→ } 331→} 332→ 333→/** 334→ * Parses the sources of the ``, `

content.thinking:
**Diagnosing script tag removal issue**

I'm exploring why parseSourcesToFileScheme strips `<script>` tags when substitutions happen. I see that parsing with Jsoup.parse builds a full document and script tags might be moved to the `<head>`, so returning only doc.body().html excludes them. Switching to Jsoup.parseBodyFragment for parsing could keep scripts inside the body and preserve them after modifications. I’m also considering test strategies to verify this fix while respecting the rule to add new test files instead of modifying existing ones.

content.thinking:
**Planning TemplateManager parse fix and new test**

I’m laying out a plan to fix TemplateManager’s parseSourcesToFileScheme by switching to Jsoup’s parseBodyFragment for better script retention, then creating a new test file to verify inline scripts remain intact while images convert properly. The test will assert key script content is preserved alongside rewritten file paths, ensuring the fix covers the reported issue without modifying existing tests.

content.text:
Summary:
- Fixed `parseSourcesToFileScheme` to parse card HTML as a body fragment so Jsoup keeps inline `<script>` sections when rewriting media URLs.
- Added a new regression test (`ParseSourcesToFileSchemeScriptTest`) that ensures inline scripts remain intact while relative sources are converted to `file:///` URIs.

Tests: Not run (not requested).

Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 49→ * 50→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 51→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 52→ * 53→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 54→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 55→ * 56→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 57→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 58→ * the filter is skipped. 59→ */ 60→class TemplateManager { 61→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 62→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 63→ companion object { 64→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 65→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 66→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 67→ 68→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 69→ } 70→ 71→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 72→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 73→ for (node in nodes) { 74→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 75→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 76→ } else { 77→ results.append( 78→ Pair( 79→ null, 80→ TemplateReplacement( 81→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 82→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 83→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 84→ ) 85→ ) 86→ ) 87→ } 88→ } 89→ 90→ return results 91→ } 92→ 93→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 94→ val value = tag.valueCase 95→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 96→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 97→ } else { 98→ TTSTag( 99→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 100→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 101→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 102→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 103→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 104→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 105→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 106→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 107→ ) 108→ } 109→ } 110→ 111→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 112→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 113→ } 114→ } 115→ } 116→ 117→ /** 118→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 119→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 120→ * using the _private fields directly. 121→ */ 122→ class TemplateRenderContext( 123→ card: Card, 124→ note: Note, 125→ browser: Boolean = false, 126→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 127→ template: JSONObject? = null, 128→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 129→ ) { 130→ private var _card: Card = card 131→ private var _note: Note = note 132→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 133→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 134→ 135→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 136→ 137→ companion object { 138→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 139→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 140→ } 141→ 142→ fun fromCardLayout( 143→ note: Note, 144→ card: Card, 145→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 146→ template: JSONObject, 147→ fillEmpty: Boolean 148→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 149→ return TemplateRenderContext( 150→ card, 151→ note, 152→ notetype = notetype, 153→ template = template, 154→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 155→ ) 156→ } 157→ } 158→ 159→ /** 160→ * Returns the card being rendered. 161→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 162→ * infinite loop. 163→ */ 164→ fun card() = _card 165→ 166→ fun note() = _note 167→ fun noteType() = noteType 168→ 169→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 170→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 171→ try { 172→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 173→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 174→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 175→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 176→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 177→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 178→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 179→ ) 180→ } 181→ 182→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 183→ val qtext = parseVideos( 184→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 185→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 186→ mediaDir 187→ ), 188→ mediaDir 189→ ) 190→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 191→ var qoutText = qout.text 192→ 193→ val atext = parseVideos( 194→ parseSourcesToFileScheme( 195→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 196→ mediaDir 197→ ), 198→ mediaDir 199→ ) 200→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 201→ var aoutText = aout.text 202→ 203→ if (!_browser) { 204→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 205→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 206→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 207→ } 208→ 209→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 210→ questionText = qoutText, 211→ answerText = aoutText, 212→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 213→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 214→ css = noteType().getString("css") 215→ ) 216→ } 217→ 218→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 219→ val proto = col.run { 220→ if (_template != null) { 221→ // card layout screen 222→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 223→ _note.toBackendNote(), 224→ _card.ord, 225→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 226→ fillEmpty, 227→ true 228→ ) 229→ } else { 230→ // existing card (eg study mode) 231→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 232→ } 233→ } 234→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 235→ } 236→ 237→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 238→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 239→ var questionText: String, 240→ var answerText: String, 241→ val questionAvTags: List, 242→ val answerAvTags: List, 243→ val css: String = "" 244→ ) { 245→ 246→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 247→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 248→ } 249→ 250→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 251→ fun applyCustomFilters( 252→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 253→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 254→ frontSide: String? 255→ ): String { 256→ // template already fully rendered? 257→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 258→ return rendered[0].first!! 259→ } 260→ 261→ var res = "" 262→ for (union in rendered) { 263→ if (union.first != null) { 264→ res += union.first!! 265→ } else { 266→ val node = union.second!! 267→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 268→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 269→ node.currentText = frontSide 270→ } 271→ 272→ var fieldText = node.currentText 273→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 274→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 275→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 276→ } 277→ } 278→ 279→ res += fieldText 280→ } 281→ } 282→ return res 283→ } 284→ } 285→ 286→ /** 287→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 288→ * 289→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 290→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 291→ */ 292→ abstract class FieldFilter { 293→ abstract fun apply( 294→ fieldText: String, 295→ fieldName: String, 296→ filterName: String, 297→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 298→ ): String 299→ } 300→ companion object { 301→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 302→ } 303→} 304→ 305→/** 306→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 307→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 308→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 309→ * we use the webview player, like AnkiMobile does 310→ * 311→ * `file:///` is used to enable seeking the video 312→ */ 313→@NotInLibAnki 314→@VisibleForTesting 315→fun parseVideos(text: String, mediaDir: String): String { 316→ return SOUND_RE.replace(text) { match -> 317→ val fileName = match.groupValues[1] 318→ val extension = fileName.substringAfterLast(".", "") 319→ if (extension in VIDEO_EXTENSIONS) { 320→ val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, fileName).toString() 321→ val uri = getFileUri(path) 322→ 323→ @Language("HTML") 324→ val result = 325→ """""" 326→ result 327→ } else { 328→ match.value 329→ } 330→ } 331→} 332→ 333→/** 334→ * Parses the sources of the ``, `
